strengths: Tight fit with seed's 'experimental utilitarian dashboard' framing — four-quadrant ops console reads as a genuine NOAA-workstation pastiche, not generic template
Seven distinct, concrete pages with differentiated layout patterns (quadrant dashboard, tabular log archive, parts catalog, CWA accordion, risk-shaded calendar, masonry+EXIF, callsign roster)
Specific motif vocabulary: packet status bar, ASCII separators, '>' quote nesting, callsign tags like KC5-WHIRL, amber WATCH/WARNING chips — avoids vagueness
Interactions are useful and motivated: comparison cart up to four items, day-cell side panel, photo lightbox with radar overlay, callsign hover-card
Required text and disclaimers ('NOT AN OFFICIAL NWS PRODUCT') reinforce community-run framing realistically
Content model is domain-true: LSR pings, hodograph snapshots, CAPE/Helicity/SRH tables, CWAs
weaknesses: Tone is uniformly terse across all pages; could risk monotony without the longer-form variation candidate B introduces
Gear comparison and directory pages, while concrete, are the more conventional pieces of the set

strengths: Split-rail console + reading column is a genuinely experimental layout invention that still honors the dashboard palette
Distinct motif assets (hodograph spiral dividers, isobar contour pattern, ASCII compass roses, bracketed callsign tags) give the site a recognizable signature

Seven concrete pages with meaningfully different patterns (bulletin split, masonry+full reading view, test-rig two-column, CB-net roster with check-in queue, seasonal timeline, curated portfolio, guild ledger)
Adds storytelling depth (narrative field logs, briefer bios, photo portfolio) the seed allows without diluting domain credibility
Net check-in queue and 'tonight's net control callsign + frequency' is a sharp, realistic detail
weaknesses: Leans slightly away from the 'very high density' seed metadata by giving real estate to a reading column
Some page goals overlap conceptually with A (calendar/briefings, directory/guild) but execution is differentiated enough to stand
'EST. 2011' required text feels arbitrary versus A's more operational required strings
